actual_polling_data.actual: 47% of women strongly disapproved of overturning Roe in post-Dobbs polling.
61% called overturning Roe a bad thing for the country.
Large majorities of Democrats said abortion should be legal in all or most cases.
Republican base support for abortion restrictions largely held firm.
Independents leaned negative and showed susceptibility to attitude shifts after Dobbs.

interpretation: The system matched broad segment-level directional reactions without claiming to replace polling.
